name: Team Beto
subject: FW: Beto’s note
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Beto for America
body:
This morning Beto sent you an important note. I wanted to make sure you saw it, especially in light of the last couple weeks.
#
This morning Beto sent you an important note about rejoining the campaign trail. I wanted to make sure you saw it below.
We’ve spent the last couple of weeks in El Paso with the campaign on pause. No fundraising emails, no campaign events. It was the right thing to do: for El Paso, for Beto, and for this campaign. We’re proud of him, and proud of this community, but we need to make up some ground.
[Take a look at Beto’s email and make a $3 donation to help us pick up our fundraising pace now that we’re back on the trail.](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/24445?refcode=ema20190815-49152&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=49152%2E444211%2EUVShtW)
\- Team Beto
* * *

The past two weeks have changed El Paso. They’ve changed America. And they’ve changed me too. These last few weeks, I made a decision to focus on El Paso and to be here for my kids and my community. A big part of me wants to stay here with all the people I love.
But this moment requires leadership. It demands we take on the man who bears so much responsibility: Donald Trump. I have to be in this fight for my kids, for El Paso, and for everyone in this country. So today we are heading back out to Mississippi today and Arkansas tomorrow because I’m thinking about this campaign in a new way.
As we get back out there, we’ll need you with us more than ever, especially since we took the last two weeks and focused exclusively on El Paso and healing instead of fundraising and campaigning.
[There is no question in my mind that this was the right thing to do, but I need you to recommit to this fight with me. Can you give our campaign $3 right now to help us make up for our fundraising gap?](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/24445?refcode=ema20190815-49152&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=20&akid=49152%2E444211%2EUVShtW)
**[Contribute $3 »](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/24445?refcode=ema20190815-49152&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=21&akid=49152%2E444211%2EUVShtW)**
I see more clearly now than I did two weeks ago that the 390 million guns in a country of 320 million, especially weapons of war, are an existential threat to this country. These guns, including the AK-47 variant that the El Paso terrorist used, are meant to be on battlefields, not here in El Paso. They’re meant to kill as many people as efficiently as possible. We cannot sell assault weapons anymore, and we need to get them off our streets.
I see more clearly than ever that we must address our failure to provide the dignity that every American deserves in their lives — from preventing economic isolation to ensuring that everyone realizes their right to health care. If we don’t, we are providing fertile ground for fascism and nativism.
I see more clearly than ever that we must be constantly telling our story — of how we got here, of who we are, of who and what makes this country great — or it will be told by those who will lie and provoke fear.
I see more clearly than ever that we must not just defend immigrants — but elevate them, speak with truth and pride about their accomplishments, and make sure no one in this country — regardless of immigration status — ever has to live in fear.
I see more clearly than ever that when we stand up against Trump and for what we believe in, we are stronger.
**Moving forward, I will fight with urgency and clarity.** I will speak as honestly as possible about the challenges we face and run a campaign that meets this moment.
When Trump terrorizes communities, like we saw last week with his ICE raids in Mississippi, we will be there. We will show up anywhere there is suffering, anywhere people are crying out to be heard. I don’t care if it’s an early state or the last state on the calendar — we will show up.
On the campaign trail, we will be a voice for communities like El Paso which have borne the brunt of Trump’s racism, hatred and division. We will carry El Paso’s strength, kindness, warmth and pride with us at all times.
Ultimately, we will campaign on the belief in an idea of America that has never fully been realized — an idea we know can only be true when all of us come together.
From the very start, we’ve funded this effort with that idea in mind. All our money has been raised from people coming together to chip in what they can. And now we need your help to make our campaign stronger than it’s ever been.
[It’s game on. Not just for this campaign, but for this country. Can you give $3 right now to make sure we can take this fight to Donald Trump? We have a fundraising gap and we need you to help us fill it.](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/24445?refcode=ema20190815-49152&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=22&akid=49152%2E444211%2EUVShtW)
Our country is in grave danger. And in this democracy, there are no sidelines to stand on. We either fight to protect America or we lose what this country means to us — and the world — forever.
I’m ready to get after this. I’m ready to fight for our future. But I can’t do it without you.
Thanks for being with us,
Beto
**[Contribute](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/24445?refcode=ema20190815-49152&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=23&akid=49152%2E444211%2EUVShtW)**

Paid for by Beto for America
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. This campaign is powered by grassroots supporters like you. But if you'd like to receive fewer emails, [you can do so here](https://act.betoorourke.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/). If you need to remove yourself from our email list, [click here to unsubscribe](https://act.betoorourke.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=24&akid=49152%2E444211%2EUVShtW). To contribute via check, please address to Beto for America campaign, P.O. Box 3628 El Paso, TX 79923.

subject: "A maverick"
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Joe Sestak for President
body:
Peter,
There is a reason **t****he latest [HarrisX/ScottRasmussen.com poll](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/9107189/89227625/-365263750?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS83NTY5NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5ZjEwNmYwOS0yZWMwLWU5MTEtYmNkMC0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=RvtXYokXfXCMXntdFD6sTuRYkA1zZ2qFmrtRJng1hDk=) has us tied for 9th among 24 Presidential candidates **after only seven weeks.
**[Contribute](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/9107190/89227626/1036341175?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS83NTY5NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5ZjEwNmYwOS0yZWMwLWU5MTEtYmNkMC0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=RvtXYokXfXCMXntdFD6sTuRYkA1zZ2qFmrtRJng1hDk=&refcode=em08162019)**
**Calling me "a maverick,”** [Vox](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/9107191/89227627/-1902359365?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS83NTY5NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5ZjEwNmYwOS0yZWMwLWU5MTEtYmNkMC0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=RvtXYokXfXCMXntdFD6sTuRYkA1zZ2qFmrtRJng1hDk=) let me explain: "What this nation most yearns for and most wants is someone who is accountable to them. We're excited to share with you that yesterday, the League of Conservation Voters Action Fund endorsed Abigail's campaign for re-election! Taking action to combat climate change and protecting our natural resources is vital to our national security and the health and well-being of Central Virginians.

clean_body: [] DEMO MEMO: This week has been a telling week for the self-serving, corrupt GOP. The West Virginia Republican Party is in chaos a week before their annual meeting with four counties trying to kick out their Chairwoman and their Governor. Also this week a self-serving GOP delegate resigned to become a lobbyist and another is in the middle of a prostitution ring investigation. But wait, there's more: Governor Jim Justice is in hot water after the Charleston Gazette-Mail released a story [] that included findings of Jim Justice lining his own pockets and the Greenbrier Resort directly benefiting from state business. The story states that, " Justice continues to profit from state business, with agencies spending more than $106,000 at his resort since he took office." This doesn't include lobbyists that wine and dine government officials OR The West Virginia Republican Party that is hosting their annual meeting there next week expecting each individual to spend hundreds of thousands of dollars to Jim Justice. Legislator to Lobbyist: Self-serving GOP Delegate Harshbarger is resigning to become a lobbyist for Dominion Energy. Yes, you read that right. [] He abandoned his constituents to continue to line his pockets with the company. He advocated for the industry during his time as Delegate and clearly always planned to leave his constituents behind. Corrupt GOP Senator Mike Maroney is being investigated for taking part in a prostitution ring in Glen Dale. His car has been searched and his cell phone has been linked,including text messages where he allegedly solicitated sex from the woman he's connected with. [] The GOP often defines their own morality, and it's clear that they are NOT the Party who abides by what they preach. The West Virginia Republican Party is heading to court. [] Infighting and rule violations have caused many counties to call for Melody Potter's resignation. Their annual meeting is next week at the Greenbrier where Justice is expected to make hundreds to thousands of dollars per member. | | The Santa Clarita Gazette recently shared an article about my background and candidacy for California's 25th Congressional District. Here are the highlights:

**"[Getting to Know Angela Underwood Jacobs](https://angelaforyou.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d7d4b85919c13e69612d61456&id=3cb6b4f04b&e=ec66c3b29a)"**
---
| **"If a congressperson is supposed to work for the people they represent, Angela Underwood Jacobs has the requisite experience."**
---
| The banker and Lancaster City Councilmember might be best known for leading the charge to draft an ordinance known as “Gabriel’s Law.” It’s named for Gabriel Fernandez, who was tortured to death by his mother and her boyfriend in 2013 when he was just eight years old. Social workers with the county Department of Children & Family Services faced criminal charges for mishandling evidence of escalating abuse. The mother pleaded guilty and was sentenced to life without parole; the boyfriend was convicted and sentenced to death. The Lancaster City Council unanimously adopted the ordinance in 2017. Now, all county social workers must digitally record visits made to Lancaster homes. “If nothing else, we are sitting at the table talking about what else we can all do better, and that is what this is about — saving lives and figuring out easier ways to get things done,” she said. Underwood Jacobs hopes to do the same for the 25th Congressional District, so she challenged Rep. Katie Hill for the seat, one of four Republicans to do so. **> >[Read More Here](https://angelaforyou.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d7d4b85919c13e69612d61456&id=b6023db1e4&e=ec66c3b29a)<<**
---
|
---
| [Help Support Angela's Bid for Congress ->>](https://angelaforyou.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d7d4b85919c13e69612d61456&id=92c9b6fb6e&e=ec66c3b29a "Help Support Angela's Bid for Congress ->>")
---
| Our strong, conservative message is getting out there, but we really need **your help** to make sure it reaches all corners of the 25th District.

Katie Hill and Nancy Pelosi are gearing up for a fight to drag our outstanding community into their socialist world of **Medicare for All** \- which could mean doctor shortages, longer wait times, and life-threatening delays in access to the latest drugs for serious diseases.

As you know, I can't win this fight alone. _We need to stick together to beat Katie Hill and her socialist allies next year._

**About Angela Underwood Jacobs:**

Underwood Jacobs currently serves as the regional Senior Vice President of California Bank and Trust, where she helps Californians live their _American Dream_ – purchase their first home, receive a loan to launch a small business, or save for retirement. #
I just need a quick moment -- Tonight at midnight is our mid-month fundraising deadline, and I wanted to tell you why this one is so important:
* Our team’s research indicates that more voters than ever are going to engage with our campaign after Labor Day, which means we need a strong operation to get our message out;

* August is notoriously one of the most difficult months for online fundraising -- and this month has been no exception;

* Our average donation is $17.40 this week -- which is a little less than our usual average gift. We’re going to need more people to step up for our campaign if we’re going to catch up on our goals.

We need to get back on track by tonight. If we’re going to win, it’s going to be because we worked together to build a real movement in this country. I know I can count on you. For The People, — Kamala Harris PAID FOR BY KAMALA HARRIS FOR THE PEOPLE

name: =?utf-8?q?Jen_O=E2=80=99Malley_Dillon=2C_Beto_for_America?=
subject: Important: Memo on Beto’s campaign
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Beto for America
body:
Today I want to chart out the state of the campaign: where we’ve been, where we are now, and where we’re going.
#
Two weeks ago we changed forever.
A man driven by hatred and racism drove 650 miles to El Paso to wreak terror on a community which has welcomed our whole team with such open hearts.
None of us will ever forget the volunteers sheltering in place. We won’t forget the way our team came together. And we won’t forget watching El Paso show us the best of America, the best of humanity, in the wake of unimaginable tragedy.
In Beto’s speech yesterday, he talked about the kind of campaign he wants to run going forward. **Now I want to chart out the state of the campaign: where we’ve been, where we are now, and where we’re going.** We paused fundraising and campaigning for two weeks so our campaign could focus on helping this community heal. Now we need your help to raise another $500,000 by the end of the month.
[Before you read on, please make a contribution to Beto towards our $500,000 goal. Refocusing our campaign the last few weeks was the right call, but now we need to up our fundraising to make up for it.](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/24726?refcode=ema20190816-49283&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=49283%2E444211%2EYnLWK4)
**Where we’ve been**
---
As soon as we knew what was happening, there was no question what we had to do. We turned our campaign’s attention on the community here in El Paso and shifted our focus to how we can help. I’m so proud of this team’s response — bringing food to victims and their families, donating blood, and raising money for the El Paso Community Fund. With your help, we raised hundreds of thousands of dollars for the victims and survivors of the attack.
Another campaign or another candidate might have taken advantage or used this moment to raise money for a campaign — and some even did. Not Beto. Not this team. We canceled our campaign schedule, and put all our efforts into raising as much money as possible for El Paso.
Following the act of terror in El Paso, our teams across the country in states like New Hampshire, South Carolina and Iowa all stood in solidarity. In New Hampshire, Team Beto decided that inaction wasn’t an option. So they started a hashtag— #RefuseToDoNothing—and lived those words.
They donated blood, protested, marched, attended vigils, made care packages — and, most importantly, supported each other. They made it clear in their community that we will rise above hate.
**Where we are now**
---
Just yesterday, I got an update on the campaign’s finances. Taking two weeks off of fundraising was the right thing to do for El Paso, for Beto, and for this campaign. But it means we lost out on a lot of time.
We’ve just suffered several of our lowest fundraising days of the campaign. Now that we’re picking back up, we need your help fast.
[Can you make a $3 contribution right now to Beto to help us close our fundraising shortfall? We need to raise $500,000 more in August to make up for two weeks’ lost time.](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/24726?refcode=ema20190816-49283&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=20&akid=49283%2E444211%2EYnLWK4)
**[Contribute $3](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/24726?refcode=ema20190816-49283&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=21&akid=49283%2E444211%2EYnLWK4)**
I wish fundraising weren’t so important, but it is. We have the resources we need to be in this for the long haul, but if we don't raise more this quarter, we'll have no choice but to make tough adjustments to our financial plan.
**Where we’re going**
---
Beto said yesterday that he’s changed his perspective on this race. This campaign has changed too.
Moving forward, we’re going to be working with a fire under us. We know better than most how urgent and important this work is. We are going to be as clear and as strong as possible in drawing our contrasts with Donald Trump.
We have what we need to win: the candidate, the team, and the strategy. And of course, our most critical asset is you. We have the best supporters who are engaged, energized, and ready to put in the work.
We’re already qualified for the fall debates because of all your amazing grassroots donations. But we can’t just rely on those debates to get our message out. We need to build a much bigger, more widespread campaign.
And to do that, we’re going to need some serious help to raise another $500,000 before the end of the month. [Can you make a $3 donation right now to help us hit that goal?](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/24726?refcode=ema20190816-49283&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=22&akid=49283%2E444211%2EYnLWK4)
Yesterday marked a new beginning for us, for this campaign, and for our country. I’m so fired up about the next few months, and so glad to be in this with you.
Thank you,
Jen O'Malley Dillon
Campaign Manager
Beto for America
**[Contribute](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/24726?refcode=ema20190816-49283&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=23&akid=49283%2E444211%2EYnLWK4)**

Paid for by Beto for America
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. This campaign is powered by grassroots supporters like you. But if you'd like to receive fewer emails, [you can do so here](https://act.betoorourke.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/). If you need to remove yourself from our email list, [click here to unsubscribe](https://act.betoorourke.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=24&akid=49283%2E444211%2EYnLWK4). To contribute via check, please address to Beto for America campaign, P.O. Box 3628 El Paso, TX 79923.

body:
|
---
[](http://secure.timryanforamerica.?g=i0wvQ1H8x8Va89sNxWeKKQ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tr-august-earlystates?refcode=TR_EM_FR_ACT_190816_1_1&amount=3&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=timryan&utm_content=1&utm_campaign=TR_EM_FR_ACT_190816_1_1&source=TR_EM_FR_ACT_190816_1_1")
CNN just interviewed Tim and he talked about his unique approach to leadership -- which focuses on the principles of mindfulness.

#
Team,
The next generation isn't waiting to fight for a future free from gun violence and they have every right to know what their elected officials are doing--or not doing--to keep them safe.
Steve Daines was held to account this week by one brave young woman who wasn't satisfied with the answers she received from Daines. She's a gun user, an outdoorswoman, and a high school student, and she knows better than Daines and Mitch McConnell that it's time for the Senate to stand up to the NRA and pass commonsense gun reforms that will keep all of us safe.
[](https://act.wilmotcollins.?g=ENsuPVNw52hc6i6rfY8XtkA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wc-email?refcode=em190816&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&employer=&occupation=")
_(Beatrice Erickson, 15, confronts Sen. Daines on his position on gun safety measures. Photo Courtesy of Erin Erickson)_
[**As Montana 's next Senator, I won't bow down the NRA and the gun lobby. I'll take meaningful action to keep our families safe. Contribute $5 to my campaign now.**](https://act.wilmotcollins.?g=ENsuPVNw52hc6i6rfY8XtkA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wc-email?refcode=em190816&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&employer=&occupation=")
I salute the brave young woman who stood up to Steve Daines this week. Like so many of us, she has had enough of inaction and excuses from the GOP. It is long past time for our leaders to take responsibility for curbing the gun violence epidemic with necessary reforms to our laws.
While all of us may not have the opportunity to demand action from Daines in person like this courageous young woman, we have the chance to hold him accountable at the ballot box.
[**I 'm running to defeat Steve Daines in 2020 because Montanans and all Americans deserve action to stop gun violence. Pitch in $5 now to help send me to the Senate.**](https://act.wilmotcollins.?g=ENsuPVNw52hc6i6rfY8XtkA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wc-email?refcode=em190816&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&employer=&occupation=")

Wilmot

name: Elizabeth Warren
subject: Washington owes Native communities a fighting chance to build stronger communities and a brighter future.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren for President
body:
Washington must do better. Read more about how we can make that happen: | | | [](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/285297ba/45c30650/128750476/668519bd/3304267495/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0=/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://elizabethwarren.com?source=em-header&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")
---
After years of failure, it's finally time for Washington to fully recognize its historic and ongoing trust and treaty obligations to Tribal Nations.

For generations, the federal government robbed Native Americans of over a billion acres of land, suppressed their languages and cultures, and literally stole children from communities in an effort to eradicate entire cultures. It's a history of violence, theft, and broken promises.

Despite this history, Tribal Nations and indigenous peoples have proven resilient, and continue to contribute to a country that took so much and keeps asking for more.

But Washington must do better, including by...
* Ending the corruption that empowers giant corporate interests to desecrate Native American sacred sites and violate Tribal treaty rights
* Protecting historic monuments and public lands
* Taking steps to stop violence against Native people, especially women and girls
* Ending the neglect that allows Congress to chip away at nutrition assistance, health care, education funding, and other programs

And to truly make big, structural change, we must ensure that America's sacred trust and treaty obligations are binding legal and moral principles -- not merely slogans.

**That 's why I've teamed up with Representative Deb Haaland, one of the first Native American women to serve in Congress, on a proposal called the Honoring Promises to Native Nations Act. And it's why I've put forward a set of additional ideas to uphold the federal government's trust and treaty obligations with Tribal Nations and to empower Native communities.**

**[Washington owes Native communities a fighting chance to build stronger communities and a brighter future. Read more here about how we can make this happen.](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/285297ba/45c30650/128750476/668519bc/3304267495/VEsE/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://medium.com/@teamwarren/honoring-and-empowering-tribal-nations-and-indigenous-peoples-720e49e1d1ca")**
| **[**READ MORE**](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/285297ba/45c30650/128750476/668519bc/3304267495/VEsF/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://medium.com/@teamwarren/honoring-and-empowering-tribal-nations-and-indigenous-peoples-720e49e1d1ca")**
---

I have fought for a brighter future for Tribal Nations during my time in the Senate, and I'm proud to come together on this proposal with Representative Haaland in the House. And when we take the White House, here's what I'll work on as president:

**1\. Guaranteeing vital resources:** Our bill will ensure predictable, guaranteed funding for vital programs for Indian Country -- no matter the circumstances in Washington. Trust and treaty obligations do not vanish because of political games, and funding these programs is not optional.

**2\. Elevating Tribal priorities to the highest levels of federal government:** I know that when it comes to government decisions, it matters who's in the room -- and what authority they have. As president, I will establish a permanent, statutory White House Council on Native American Affairs and create a new White House Budgetary Office of Tribal Affairs to track government-wide progress on Native and indigenous programs.

**3\. Economic development:** We will invest in the physical, digital, and financial infrastructure that Native communities need to sustain economic development. From ensuring access to safe drinking water to building fiber broadband connections, it is time for new government policies to empower Native families to thrive economically.

**4\. New economic opportunities:** We also need to respect tribal jurisdiction over tribal businesses and promote forward-looking efforts to ensure full access to new economic opportunities. For example, a number of Tribal Nations view cannabis as an important economic opportunity. I support full marijuana legalization and have advanced the STATES Act, a proposal that would safeguard the ability of Tribal Nations to make their own marijuana policies.

**5\. Housing:** The housing crisis in Indian Country isn't new, but its severity is shocking. In far too many places, extreme overcrowding is the norm. My housing plan would substantially expand the Indian Housing Block Grant to $2.5 billion and build or rehabilitate 200,000 homes.

**6\. Health care:** I am calling for a new commitment to Native health -- one that replaces the current, chronically underfunded regime with one that guarantees funding for all necessary services, ensures access to adequate medical providers throughout Tribal lands, and includes specific programs designed to zero in on behavioral health and chronic disease issues. And when we achieve Medicare for All, coverage disparities for Native populations can be eliminated.

We also need to ensure new resources to combat substance use disorder, empower tribes to resolve the suicide crisis, and address childhood trauma. I have advanced several legislative proposals to address these issues, from the CARE ACT to the American Indian and Alaska Native Child Abuse Prevention and Treatment Act.

**7\. Education:** We will involve Tribal governments as the local administrators of Universal Child Care, fully fund Bureau of Indian Education Schools, and ensure that all students understand the important place Native Nations occupy in American history. Our plan for universal free college would also make it possible for students at Tribal Colleges and Universities to graduate without paying a dime in tuition or fees.

**8\. Honoring Native veterans:** Native Americans serve in the military at among the highest rates in the country. We should honor this service and guarantee that Native veterans receive the services they have earned, including housing assistance to combat the disproportionate risk of homelessness for Native veterans.

**9\. Public safety and criminal justice:** Washington has refused to fully respect tribal sovereignty in criminal justice matters. This has deprived countless crime victims of the opportunity to get justice and fed the ongoing epidemic of missing and murdered indigenous women.

In 2013, the Violence Against Women Act crucially recognized some criminal jurisdiction by Tribal Nations, but we need to go further. I support a full Oliphant fix to fully recognize the inherent jurisdiction of tribes over their sovereign territory and end the circumstances that effectively immunize crimes on Tribal lands. And we need to provide tribal authorities sufficient funding to provide robust legal systems that deliver justice for victims and due process to criminal defendants.

**10\. Tribal lands and tribal sovereignty:** Washington must stop putting the interests of companies that want to exploit our environment ahead of the interests of Native people who seek to preserve their sacred sites. As president, I will expand federally protected land that is important to tribes and protect historic monuments and sacred sites from companies that see it as just another place to drill or mine. I will revoke the improperly granted permits for the Keystone XL and Dakota Access pipelines and make respect for sacred tribal religious interests the law of the land by passing a new Sacred Lands Religious Freedom Restoration Act.

**11\. Voting rights:** We must ensure that Native people have full and equal access to our democracy -- including making voting convenient and secure and wiping out laws that suppress the vote. I have proposed sweeping reforms to achieve this, and will continue advocating for the Native American Voting Rights Act to combat festering discriminatory practices.

**These proposals aren 't the last word on everything we'll need to do. As president, I'm committed to continuing to engage and work with Native communities -- to make sure we do everything we can to support Tribal Nations and indigenous peoples.**

**[And we'll fight side by side as a grassroots movement to live up to our commitments as a country. You can read more details on these proposals here.](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/285297ba/45c30650/128750476/668519bc/3304267495/VEsC/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://medium.com/@teamwarren/honoring-and-empowering-tribal-nations-and-indigenous-peoples-720e49e1d1ca")**

Thanks for being a part of this,

Elizabeth

clean_body: Washington must do better. Read more about how we can make that happen: | | | "" After years of failure, it's finally time for Washington to fully recognize its historic and ongoing trust and treaty obligations to Tribal Nations. For generations, the federal government robbed Native Americans of over a billion acres of land, suppressed their languages and cultures, and literally stole children from communities in an effort to eradicate entire cultures. It's a history of violence, theft, and broken promises. Despite this history, Tribal Nations and indigenous peoples have proven resilient, and continue to contribute to a country that took so much and keeps asking for more. But Washington must do better, including by... Ending the corruption that empowers giant corporate interests to desecrate Native American sacred sites and violate Tribal treaty rights Protecting historic monuments and public lands Taking steps to stop violence against Native people, especially women and girls Ending the neglect that allows Congress to chip away at nutrition assistance, health care, education funding, and other programs And to truly make big, structural change, we must ensure that America's sacred trust and treaty obligations are binding legal and moral principles -- not merely slogans. That 's why I've teamed up with Representative Deb Haaland, one of the first Native American women to serve in Congress, on a proposal called the Honoring Promises to Native Nations Act. And it's why I've put forward a set of additional ideas to uphold the federal government's trust and treaty obligations with Tribal Nations and to empower Native communities. "" | "" I have fought for a brighter future for Tribal Nations during my time in the Senate, and I'm proud to come together on this proposal with Representative Haaland in the House. And when we take the White House, here's what I'll work on as president: 1\. Guaranteeing vital resources: Our bill will ensure predictable, guaranteed funding for vital programs for Indian Country -- no matter the circumstances in Washington. Trust and treaty obligations do not vanish because of political games, and funding these programs is not optional. 2\. Elevating Tribal priorities to the highest levels of federal government: I know that when it comes to government decisions, it matters who's in the room -- and what authority they have. As president, I will establish a permanent, statutory White House Council on Native American Affairs and create a new White House Budgetary Office of Tribal Affairs to track government-wide progress on Native and indigenous programs. 3\. Economic development: We will invest in the physical, digital, and financial infrastructure that Native communities need to sustain economic development. From ensuring access to safe drinking water to building fiber broadband connections, it is time for new government policies to empower Native families to thrive economically. 4\. New economic opportunities: We also need to respect tribal jurisdiction over tribal businesses and promote forward-looking efforts to ensure full access to new economic opportunities. For example, a number of Tribal Nations view cannabis as an important economic opportunity. I support full marijuana legalization and have advanced the STATES Act, a proposal that would safeguard the ability of Tribal Nations to make their own marijuana policies. 5\. Housing: The housing crisis in Indian Country isn't new, but its severity is shocking. In far too many places, extreme overcrowding is the norm. My housing plan would substantially expand the Indian Housing Block Grant to $2.5 billion and build or rehabilitate 200,000 homes. 6\. Health care: I am calling for a new commitment to Native health -- one that replaces the current, chronically underfunded regime with one that guarantees funding for all necessary services, ensures access to adequate medical providers throughout Tribal lands, and includes specific programs designed to zero in on behavioral health and chronic disease issues. And when we achieve Medicare for All, coverage disparities for Native populations can be eliminated. We also need to ensure new resources to combat substance use disorder, empower tribes to resolve the suicide crisis, and address childhood trauma. I have advanced several legislative proposals to address these issues, from the CARE ACT to the American Indian and Alaska Native Child Abuse Prevention and Treatment Act. 7\. Education: We will involve Tribal governments as the local administrators of Universal Child Care, fully fund Bureau of Indian Education Schools, and ensure that all students understand the important place Native Nations occupy in American history. Our plan for universal free college would also make it possible for students at Tribal Colleges and Universities to graduate without paying a dime in tuition or fees. 8\. Honoring Native veterans: Native Americans serve in the military at among the highest rates in the country. We should honor this service and guarantee that Native veterans receive the services they have earned, including housing assistance to combat the disproportionate risk of homelessness for Native veterans. 9\. Public safety and criminal justice: Washington has refused to fully respect tribal sovereignty in criminal justice matters. This has deprived countless crime victims of the opportunity to get justice and fed the ongoing epidemic of missing and murdered indigenous women. In 2013, the Violence Against Women Act crucially recognized some criminal jurisdiction by Tribal Nations, but we need to go further. I support a full Oliphant fix to fully recognize the inherent jurisdiction of tribes over their sovereign territory and end the circumstances that effectively immunize crimes on Tribal lands. And we need to provide tribal authorities sufficient funding to provide robust legal systems that deliver justice for victims and due process to criminal defendants. 10\. Tribal lands and tribal sovereignty: Washington must stop putting the interests of companies that want to exploit our environment ahead of the interests of Native people who seek to preserve their sacred sites. As president, I will expand federally protected land that is important to tribes and protect historic monuments and sacred sites from companies that see it as just another place to drill or mine. I will revoke the improperly granted permits for the Keystone XL and Dakota Access pipelines and make respect for sacred tribal religious interests the law of the land by passing a new Sacred Lands Religious Freedom Restoration Act. 11\. Voting rights: We must ensure that Native people have full and equal access to our democracy -- including making voting convenient and secure and wiping out laws that suppress the vote. I have proposed sweeping reforms to achieve this, and will continue advocating for the Native American Voting Rights Act to combat festering discriminatory practices. These proposals aren 't the last word on everything we'll need to do. As president, I'm committed to continuing to engage and work with Native communities -- to make sure we do everything we can to support Tribal Nations and indigenous peoples. "" Thanks for being a part of this, Elizabeth "" | | | "" | "" | "" | "" | "" | "" ---|---|---|---|---|--- | Paid for by Warren for President All content (C) 2019 Warren for President, All Rights Reserved PO Box 171375, Boston, MA 02116

subject: BREAKING: Toss up
body:
This is anyone's race.
# [](https://gideon.bsd.net/page/m/65802dc4/4bef7a87/699689c1/287f6f8b/4055567821/VEsH/?g=siAzLVH8x8VdU6OjQM8XC0A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sg-email-template?refcode=em_header")

Friends -
When we launched, _Cook Political Report_ had our race in the "leans Republican" column.
**Less than two months later, they just labeled our race a "toss up."**
This is a great sign for our campaign - Sara's message is clearly resonating with Mainers. But here's the sticking point:
Mitch McConnell and Republican donors are reading this news too, and they're getting ready to flood Susan Collins' campaign with _even more_ large checks.
Control of the Senate is at stake, so they'll spend whatever it takes to stop our momentum. But we won't let them:
[**Quickly, make a $3 contribution to help us build on this momentum and elect Sara Gideon to the U.S. Senate.**](https://gideon.bsd.net/page/m/65802dc4/4bef7a87/699689c1/287f6f8a/4055567821/VEsE/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0xOTA4MTYtZnVsbCJ9/?g=siAzLVH8x8VdU6OjQM8XC0A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sg-august?refcode=em190816-full")
We are gaining ground because of supporters like you. If we keep this going until Election Day, we will win this race.
All our best,
\- Team Sara

name: Cory Booker
subject: re: Tom Steyer
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cory 2020
body:
| | |
---
| Team, earlier this week it was announced that Tom Steyer is one poll away from making the September debate stage. He announced his candidacy just last month and since then, he's spent $10 million of his own money to fund his campaign. The truth is, I don't have billions of dollars of personal wealth. I rely on grassroots donations of $3, $5, and $20 from people like you to power this campaign. **[If you want to be part of a movement that's being built from the grassroots up, please, donate now.](https://act.corybooker.?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cory2020-20190816-em2?refcode=em_fr_20190816_CB_20190816-fr-CB-Steyer-actives_actives_L1&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=")** In just 34 days, Tom's leapfrogged over a dozen candidates and is on track to join me and _eight_ others in qualifying for the next round of debates. I'll be honest that's going to be hard to compete with. It's why I'm asking for your help to make sure we have all the resources we need to hire field organizers, open campaign offices, and even run local ads to spread our campaign message. **[So here's my ask: If you've found yourself agreeing with anything I've said over the past six months, donate. Make a contribution today to amplify our message nationwide with grassroots dollars.](https://act.corybooker.?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cory2020-20190816-em2?refcode=em_fr_20190816_CB_20190816-fr-CB-Steyer-actives_actives_L2&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=")** Thank you,

body:
| [](conoleforcongress.com)

| | As reported by _Five Thirty Eight_ and _The Auburn Citizen_ , Republicans in competitive districts are retiring in waves ahead of the 2020 election.

**As one of only two Republicans who serve in districts won by Hillary Clinton in 2016, there has been a lot of speculation lately that my opponent, John Katko, will retire.**

This would be great news. Katko consistently votes with Donald Trump against getting big money out of politics and refuses to do anything to challenge immoral drug prices that take advantage of our sick neighbors.

[**Since we are taking no corporate PAC money, your grassroots donations are critical to financing our mission. We need to raise $5,000 for our Retirement Watch Fund by Sunday to reach our goal. body:
There’s no doubt that Indiana’s farmers are the backbone of not just Indiana, but the world. Afterall, they feed us all from nearly 60,000 farms... | | | [View this email in your browser](https://us11.campaign-archive.com/?e=1acd9bb3d6&u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=f13fb22be9)

| |
---
|
---
| **_Indiana Ag Comes Together_** There’s no doubt that Indiana’s farmers are the backbone of not just Indiana, but the world. Afterall, they feed us all from nearly 60,000 farms scattered throughout the state on more than 14 million cultivated acres. That’s a lot of corn, soybeans, pork, poultry, dairy, etc. ($31.2 billion toward our state’s GDP annually, to be precise).

And recently, we had the chance to bring together many heroes from Indiana’s agriculture community – our farmers, innovators, and business leaders – under one roof for a very special event. It was such an honor to join **Indiana Farm Bureau President Randy Kron** and his family at their farm near Evansville for a reception supporting **Governor Holcomb**. Randy and his family work hard every day to produce top yields, just like Governor Holcomb is working hard every day to deliver top results for Hoosiers.

And while there’s so much to celebrate with Indiana agriculture, it’s certainly been a challenging year for our Hoosier farmers. Faced with heavy rains during planting season, we’re all watching carefully to see how this year’s crops continue to develop. That’s why Governor Holcomb [requested an agriculture disaster designation](mailto:http://www.journalgazette.net/article/20190712/WEB/190719941) for 88 of Indiana’s counties impacted by flooding, providing a needed lifeline to our farmers in need.

So, when you have a chance – join me in thanking our farmers and our agriculture community. They’re our backbone, they’re our heroes and they’re (literally) growing Indiana!

**_PHOTO: Governor Eric Holcomb_** grabs a photo with **Indiana Farm Bureau President Randy Kron** and his family during a recent reception bringing together agriculture leaders from across Indiana!
---
|
---
| | **Governor Holcomb's Week That Was **

|
---
|
---
---
| **_Indiana State Fair: The Final Week_** It may be the final week of the Indiana State Fair, but the action hasn’t slowed down! Governor Holcomb had a busy day at the State Fair on Wednesday.
* First, he hosted White House Office of National Drug Control Policy (ONDCP) Director Jim Carroll...
* He then stopped by the Indiana Republican Party booth to meet with Hoosiers enjoying the fair...
* And then he capped off the day by attending the fair’s annual Harvest Dinner, which helps support the Indiana State Fair Foundation and its philanthropic work!

Did you have a chance to enjoy the State Fair (or are you planning to attend this weekend?)?

|
---
|
---
---
|
---
---
| **_Indiana GOP Booth at the State Fair_**
If you’re planning to attend the Indiana State Fair this weekend (the LAST weekend of the fair), make sure that you stop by the Indiana Republican Party booth! We’re located between the Covered Bridge and the Free Stage, and we’ve got your connection to _your_ Republican Party at the fair! This includes opportunities to grab a selfie with **President Donald Trump** and **Vice President Mike Pence’s** cardboard cutouts, and sign a petition to make sure that **President Trump** and **Governor Holcomb** get on the ballot next year.

And you never know who you’ll see out at the fair booth! This week, we had stops by **Governor Holcomb **and **Lt. Governor Suzanne Crouch **– great opportunities to spend time with your elected officials!

|

|
---
| **_Save the Date: Team Holcomb Fall Dinner_** You’re going to want to save this date – October 14, 2019. At this year’s Team Holcomb Fall Dinner, the theme once again is Celebrating Our Military. We’ve got another great event planned for you with more details coming next week, so make sure that you save the date!

Support **Governor Holcomb** at an exciting evening reception with both **Governor Eric Holcomb **and Indiana Republican Party **Chairman Kyle Hupfer**** ** later this month!
[RSVP now for the G.O.Party](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=9fba9b1aa7&e=1acd9bb3d6)!
Enjoy light drinks and snacks, live music from the band Password Reset, and celebrate with your fellow Hoosier Republicans. This big event will take place on Wednesday, August 28 at the Dallara IndyCar Factory in Speedway, Indiana!

subject: Back on the trail

body:
Being off the campaign trail for two weeks has put us at a serious disadvantage...
#
Yesterday, Beto returned to the campaign after spending two weeks back in El Paso, helping our community to heal.
While focusing on El Paso was absolutely the right thing to do, being off the campaign trail for two weeks has put the campaign at a disadvantage.
In fact, our finance team crunched the numbers and we need to raise **$467,000 more by the end of**** _this_****month** to close our fundraising gap.
[Can you contribute $3 today to help us reach our goal and get our campaign back on track?](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/26218?refcode=ema20190816-49330&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=19&akid=49330%2E444211%2EqdqytP)

As Beto said yesterday, you’ll find us in the places where Donald Trump has terrorized, terrified, and demeaned our fellow Americans.
Donald Trump has attacked so many communities -- and it’s important to Beto that we try to visit as many as possible. That means a lot of travel that our campaign has to fund. But, we’ll be there because we know that, when we come together to stand up against Trump, we are stronger.
[Make a $3 gift and help us reach $467,000 before the end of the month so we can take our campaign to where we’re needed the most.](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/26218?refcode=ema20190816-49330&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=21&akid=49330%2E444211%2EqdqytP)

subject: this is serious
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Weld 2020 Presidential Campaign Committee · United States
body:
|
---
[](https://www.weld2020.org/r?u=sTrCoaT-YmDhY88aC-xe_3F5VNQquXw1tKM69PB7U21uF6k4eqf2zBvwZUYjk6-hRqkeppOf4jcemetErYOC-MIHwpGyJuz3kEyC8ANeARylUXwn_67-WBBgKKMGjQSh&e=6bbde6f28245738982c2a8d24644af48&utm_source=weld2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=20190816_fr&n=1) Friend — President Trump took the MAGA circus to Manchester, New Hampshire yesterday and broke out the greatest hits. From insulting protesters, to ‘‘Lock her up” chants, to nonsensical tirades, yesterday’s rally was everything you’d expect from the President’s reelection campaign. But there was one comment Trump made that stuck with us. Before heading to the event, **Trump started laying the groundwork to invalidate next year’s election** by claiming that “thousands and thousands of people came in from locations unknown” to vote in New Hampshire in 2016. This might seem like Trump’s usual “blame everyone but himself” style, but we think he’s up to something more sinister. Here’s why: We’re making major headway in New Hampshire, **we know this because Trump is _already_ campaigning there**. Something that’s almost unheard of for an incumbent president this early in the cycle. We’re building a campaign there that’s going to cut through Trump’s bluster and reach voters where they are. **But his comments yesterday demonstrate that if we win that primary, he’s going to do everything in his power to invalidate the results, halting our momentum and _casting doubt on our democratic process._** President Trump is using the two-bit dictator playbook of “winning” elections. And his attacks on our election systems are only going to get worse as the primary gets closer. So if we want to build a movement that cuts through Trump’s megalomania, wins New Hampshire, and reclaims the Republican party, we’re going to need your help. [**Can you rush a $20.20 donation right now to help us build the organization we need to defend our democratic process?**](https://www.weld2020.org/r?u=sTrCoaT-YmDhY88aC-xe_3F5VNQquXw1tKM69PB7U21uF6k4eqf2zBvwZUYjk6-hRqkeppOf4jcemetErYOC-MIHwpGyJuz3kEyC8ANeARylUXwn_67-WBBgKKMGjQSh&e=6bbde6f28245738982c2a8d24644af48&utm_source=weld2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=20190816_fr&n=2) — Team Weld | | [DONATE ➞](https://www.weld2020.org/r?u=sTrCoaT-YmDhY88aC-xe_3F5VNQquXw1tKM69PB7U21uF6k4eqf2zBvwZUYjk6-hRqkeppOf4jcemetErYOC-MIHwpGyJuz3kEyC8ANeARylUXwn_67-WBBgKKMGjQSh&e=6bbde6f28245738982c2a8d24644af48&utm_source=weld2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=20190816_fr&n=3)

name: Kamala Harris
subject: First, an explanation --
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY KAMALA HARRIS FOR THE PEOPLE
body:
|
#
In just a moment, I am going to ask you to make a donation to my presidential campaign before tonight’s deadline. I hope you’ll give me a chance to explain why this request -- especially today -- is so important. Here is the truth: when this deadline expires, there’s going to be a budget meeting with our senior staff and they are going to evaluate whether or not we’re on pace to reach our goals for August. If we reach this goal tonight -- and it’s going to be close -- then we’ll be in our strongest position to win the primary and defeat Trump in the general. If we fall short, we might have to make some tough calls that could jeopardize all the progress we’ve made in critical early states. So with time running out, our job is to do everything we can to close this potential shortfall before midnight. That’s why I’m asking: **Rush a contribution to our campaign ahead of tonight’s mid-month fundraising deadline and help ensure our team doesn’t have to make any tough budget decisions this weekend.** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | | [Chip in $5 now](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kdh-ema-190816?refcode=ema190816-4-full&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Englewood&state=FL&zip=34223&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amount=5&express_lane=true&t=1&refcode2=5979_5755789_jiydFG&akid=5979%2E5755789%2EjiydFG)
